ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific peach-flavoured green tea entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers tea-based beverages broadly; no peach-flavoured green tea sub-code exists. Closest defensible match documented.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one cup), equivalent to a typical takeaway cup size served at Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Peach Green Tea (No Topping) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Peach Green Tea (No Topping), CR ID D08010089. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sugar-sweetened tea beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peach-flavoured green tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on sucrose-sweetened tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving calculated as: GI (55) × available carbohydrate per serving (18.7 g) ÷ 100 = 10.3. Carbohydrate per serving = 7.49 g/100g × 250 g = 18.73 g. A GI of 55 is considered low.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.49 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation, per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage; the 7.49 g/100 g sugar load exceeds the SSB threshold of >5 g/100 g and independently elevates blood pressure risk regardless of the low sodium content (1 mg/100 g) — choose plain unsweetened green tea instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49 g sugar/100 g and 0 g dietary fibre, producing rapid glucose absorption and postprandial glycaemic excursion; classified as unsuitable per ADA Standards of Medical Care 2023.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes — the 7.49 g/100 g sugar with zero fibre causes rapid glucose absorption; a 250 ml serving delivers approximately 18.7 g of free sugar with a glycaemic load of 10.3, and plain unsweetened green tea should be substituted.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk through dyslipidaemia, insulin resistance, and elevated triglycerides, per AHA 2021 dietary guidance.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage; the 7.49 g/100 g added sugar contributes to triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance — plain unsweetened green tea, which contains cardioprotective catechins (EGCG), is the preferred alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (7.49 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity and negates the anti-inflammatory EGCG polyphenol benefit of green tea.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage as the added peach syrup sugar load promotes inflammatory mediators; plain unsweetened green tea is preferred to obtain EGCG anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Green Tea (No Topping) is a sugar-sweetened speciality tea beverage popular in Singapore, consisting of freshly brewed green tea combined with peach-flavoured syrup and no additional customisable toppings such as pearls or jelly. The beverage has a light, mildly sweet, and fruity flavour profile with a thin, watery texture characteristic of brewed tea. It is served chilled or at room temperature and is commonly available at Singaporean bubble tea chains and speciality tea stalls. Per 100 ml, it provides 29 kcal, 7.49 g of carbohydrate (entirely as sugar), 0.01 g of protein, 0 g of fat, and 1 mg of sodium. The absence of toppings reduces caloric density compared to full-topping variants but does not alter the sugar-sweetened base. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with 7.49 g sugar/100 g, exceeding the SSB threshold of >5 g/100 g. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid glucose absorption, and raises blood pressure risk in hypertensive individuals independent of its low sodium content. The green tea base contains EGCG catechins with antioxidant properties, but the added sugar load negates anti-inflammatory benefit for rheumatoid arthritis. Plain unsweetened green tea is the recommended alternative for health-conscious consumers. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010089).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact product (CR ID D08010089) with a reported confidence of 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate documented with approved source basis.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database — this is a water-based brewed tea beverage containing no fat by formulation (confirmed zero). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same rationale. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as brewed tea beverages contain no intact dietary fibre after brewing and filtration (confirmed zero). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific peach green tea beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010089 as of 2026. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for peach-flavoured green tea was found in approved sources. Glycaemic load per 250 ml serving = 10.3 (GI 55 × 18.73 g carbohydrate ÷ 100). FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are approximate matches as no exact ontology entry for peach-flavoured green tea exists.
